response.justification: This candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals across diverse domains, but lacks critical mandatory requirements for the role. C++ development experience ended 10+ years ago (National Instruments, 2014-2015); recent 5+ years have focused on web/full-stack work (TypeScript, Node.js, React). Most critically absent: Windows platform experience, SQL Server/Oracle depth, and any financial services or capital markets domain expertise—all mandatory for a mid-senior capital markets position. The profile trajectory diverges significantly from role requirements.
strengths: 20 years of software engineering experience across diverse platforms (web, mobile, embedded, desktop) demonstrates strong fundamentals and language adaptability
Excellent communication skills: technical talks, workshops, mentoring roles, and strong professional brand show ability to articulate complex concepts
CI/CD and infrastructure experience including GitLab setup, deploy automation, and DevOps tooling relevant to enterprise environments
concerns: C++ development was 10+ years ago; recent career trajectory (last 5 years) is entirely web/full-stack TypeScript and Node.js with no return to systems-level languages
No Windows platform development experience mentioned anywhere in resume; career heavily weighted toward web, mobile, and Linux/Unix systems
Zero financial services, capital markets, investment banking, or fixed income domain knowledge—a mandatory requirement that cannot be substituted

key_factors.factor: Outdated C++ and missing Windows platform expertise
Complete absence of financial services domain experience
Strong foundational engineering skills and adaptability
